title: Cardiac Muscle, Conduction System, and Cardiac Cycle

questions.text: What structural feature allows cardiac muscle cells to contract as a single coordinated unit?
During phase 0 of the cardiac action potential, which ion rapidly moves into the cell?
The plateau phase (phase 2) of the action potential is characterized by which combination of ion movements?

answers.text: Rapid Na+ influx and rapid K+ efflux
Slow Ca2+ entry and slow K+ leaving
Rapid Ca2+ efflux and rapid Na+ influx
No ion movement occurs during the plateau

questions.text: During the absolute refractory period, the cardiac cell:

answers.text: Can respond to a stronger than normal stimulus
Will not respond to further stimulation
Repolarizes back to resting membrane potential

questions.text: The R on T phenomenon is most likely to occur during which period?
Which structure is the primary pacemaker of the heart with an intrinsic rate of 60-100 bpm?
What is the significance of the delay at the AV node?

answers.text: It prevents the heart from beating too fast
It allows the atria to fully empty blood into the ventricles
It increases the force of ventricular contraction
It allows the lungs to oxygenate the blood before ejection

questions.text: The QRS complex marks what electrical event on the ECG?
A patient has a cardiac output of 4.9 L/min and a heart rate of 70 bpm. What is their stroke volume?
According to the Frank-Starling law, an increase in preload leads to:

answers.text: A weaker contraction due to overstretching
A stronger contraction due to greater stretch
